headline: Elon Musk summoned by French prosecutors after tense 15-month probe into X
description: Paris prosecutors have been investigating a wide range of alleged abuses by X and its owner since January 2025. The billionaire has been summoned for a voluntary interview on Monday.

headline: US and Iran in diplomatic deadlock and naval escalation as ceasefire nears expiration
French conservatives select Bruno Retailleau as their presidential candidate
Bulgaria headed for political stability as former president wins parliamentary majority
description: With about 130 out of 240 seats in Parliament, Rumen Radev has secured an outright majority in a country marked by deep political fragmentation in recent years. He will become the new prime minister, a position more powerful than president in Bulgaria.

headline: Japan issues tsunami warning after 7.5 magnitude earthquake
description: The quake hit at 4:53 pm local time on Monday in Pacific waters off northern Iwate prefecture and the tremor was strong enough to shake large buildings as far as Tokyo, hundreds of kilometers away.

headline: Nathalie Baye, acclaimed French actress with vast filmography
description: Throughout her career, Baye intentionally took on a wide range of lead and supporting roles, portraying upper-class women to sex workers in both comedies and romantic dramas. She died on Friday at the age of 77 after a battle with Lewy body disease.

headline: 'Trump's rejection of Oman's mediation reveals his drive for war with Iran'
description: The White House has never appreciated Oman's preference for diplomacy over military force to ensure its security, writes historian Jean-Pierre Filiu in his column.

Ireland, the weak link in Europe's defense
description: Irish neutrality, long seen as a guarantee of independence from its former British colonizer, has been eroding under European and Atlantic pressures. After decades of naval weakness, Dublin has committed to strengthening its navy.

headline: A photographic journey into the daily lives of home care aides
description: Between fall 2024 and summer 2025, photographer Vincent Gouriou followed several home health aides in Dordogne, southwest France, documenting their often difficult daily routines as they traveled to isolated homes, provided care and offered a listening ear.

headline: Can we detox our bodies from pollutants like 'forever chemicals'?
description: Scientists are testing methods to remove certain pollutants from the human body. These methods can be both invasive and costly, and do not prevent renewed exposure to the most widespread environmental toxins.
